items.summary: Alcohol may increase the risk of lactic acidosis when combined with metformin; advise caution and discuss with a clinician.
items.recommendation: Limit alcohol intake; seek clinician advice especially with liver/kidney issues.

items.summary: Increased bleeding risk when NSAIDs are used with warfarin.
items.recommendation: Avoid or consult clinician; consider alternatives and monitoring.

items.summary: Potential serotonin syndrome risk when combining SSRIs with St. John's wort.
Calcium can reduce levothyroxine absorption if taken close together.
items.recommendation: Separate doses by at least 4 hours; consult clinician for personalized guidance.

items.summary: No clinically significant interaction is expected for typical use.
items.recommendation: No special action required; confirm with clinician if you have specific concerns.
